title: 200  How can vedas describe Krishna who is adhokshaja
speaker: HH Stoka Krishna Maharaj

segments.text: Now, the first task of Brahma to give every living entity the opportunity for material
enjoyment is easily done through systematic process of creation.
Brahma is empowered to do that very systematically.
But the second function of Brahma to give an opportunity for self-realization to all
the conditioned souls, that is the difficult part.
Because different living entities have got different kinds of inclinations.
They have to be offered different methods of self-realization.
Among the methods of self-realization, the method of realizing the self by cultivation
This is one of the methods, cultivation of transcendental knowledge, Jnana Marga.
This method is a very difficult method.
To explain the truth about the spirit-soul through descriptions, theoretical descriptions
Because whatever words are used to describe the spirit-soul are all words which are used
to describe the spirit-soul which are generated by the letters of the alphabet which belong
The Lord has got a name, Adhokshaja.
The Sanskrit alphabet begins with the letter A and ends with the letter Ksha.
So that which is described using the letters of the Sanskrit alphabet, that which is perceivable
through our material senses and mind is called Akshaja.
The Lord is not perceived through material descriptions.
Therefore the Lord is called beyond the perception.
So how can somebody describe something which is beyond the perception, material perception,
generated through combinations of material sounds,
You are trying to describe something
which cannot be described by material words.
which are generated out of these
53 characters of the Sanskrit alphabet which are all material sound vibrations.

Still, they are transcendental sound vibrations because the Lord, the supremely omnipotent Lord, out of his causeless mercy, he infuses transcendence into such words which ordinarily are unable to describe transcendence.
So, it is because of the Lord's mercy that the sound vibrations of the Vedic hymns are describing transcendence.
That is, ordinary material sound cannot describe anything transcendental.
But, one empowered by the Lord,
one empowered to vibrate certain sound vibrations can describe clearly what is transcendence.
